verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is a canonical nuclear DNA-binding protein without intrinsic membrane anchors (no TM domain, no GPI, no leader sequence). However, it shows documented surface accessibility exclusively under non-baseline conditions: (1) cell-death-induced (apoptosis, necrosis, pyroptosis)—HMGB1 translocates to cytoplasm and is released via vesicular fusion with PM; (2) immune-cell activation—activated macrophages/monocytes actively secrete HMGB1 via secretory lysosomes; (3) cancer contexts—cytoplasmic/membrane accumulation in tumor cells. The protein body reaches the outer face only during these stress/death/activation states, via unconventional secretory machinery, not as a baseline resident. No documented wash-resistant PM anchor; release depends on vesicle-mediated or cell-lytic mechanisms.
